name: Sa-Teh on Skewers
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Combination of boneless chicken, beef and pork
1/2 tsp Seeded and finely chopped red chili peppers
1/2 to 1 tsp. Fish sauce or 1/4 tsp. Salt
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken, beef, and pork thinly into 2-inch strips.
2. In a food processor or blender, combine oil, lemon grass, garlic, red chili peppers, curry powder, sugar, and fish sauce; blend until smooth.
3. Pour the marinade over the chicken and meat; marinate for at least 2 hours.
4. Thread the meat onto skewers and barbecue or broil, turning occasionally until cooked.
5. Serve with Sa-teh Sauce and Cucumber Sauce.
Sa-teh Sauce:
1/4 cup Oil
2 Cloves garlic, minced
1/2 to 1 tsp. ground dried red chili peppers
3 Kaffir lime leaves
1/2 tsp Curry powder
1 tbsp Chopped fresh lemon grass
1 cup Coconut milk
1/2 cup Milk
2-inch cinnamon stick
3 Bay leaves
2 tsp Tamarind paste
1 to 3 Tbsp Fish sauce
3 tbsp Dark brown sugar
3 tbsp Lemon juice
1 cup Chunky peanut butter
Cucumber Sauce:
1 Cucumber (preferably)
5 tbsp Sugar
1 cup Boiling water
1/2 cup White vinegar
1 tsp Salt
3 to 5 red chili peppers, seeded and finely chopped
3 Shallots, finely chopped
6 to 8 sprigs Chinese parsley (for garnish)
Instructions for Sa-teh Sauce:
1. Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Sauté garlic, onion, chili peppers, kaffir lime leaves, curry powder, and lemon grass for 2 to 3 minutes.
3. Stir in coconut milk, milk, cinnamon stick, bay leaves, tamarind paste, fish sauce, brown sugar, lemon juice, and peanut butter; mix well.
4. Reduce heat and cook, stirring frequently, until the sauce thickens, about 30 minutes. Be careful not to let the sauce stick to the bottom of the pan.
Instructions for Cucumber Sauce:
1. Thinly slice cucumber.
2. Dissolve sugar in boiling water; stir in white vinegar and salt.
3. Pour the sauce over cucumber slices.
4. Sprinkle with red chili peppers and shallots.
5. Chill.
6. Garnish with Chinese parsley.
